title: When AI Makes Mistakes
concept: Failure case analysis is the systematic study of a model's incorrect predictions to improve its design.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the main benefit of Active Learning?

options.text: It minimizes labeling costs by focusing human effort on the most confusing images
It speeds up model inference frames-per-second
It automatically cleans label noise without human verification

exercises.explain: Active learning targets uncertain images, saving annotation budget while maintaining accuracy.

cards.title: Analyzing the crashes
cards.body: When a self-driving car crashes, engineers don't just say 'well, the model is 98% accurate overall!' and move on. They analyze the exact circumstances of the failure to prevent it from ever happening again. In this unit, we learn how to debug vision models by studying their mistakes.

cards.title: Failure Case Analysis
cards.body: Failure Case Analysis is a qualitative review process where engineers inspect the specific inputs that caused a model to fail. By categorizing errors, they identify systemic model weaknesses and data gaps.

cards.title: An AI Detective
cards.body: Imagine a detective investigating a crime scene. They don't just look at city-wide crime statistics (which are like overall accuracy scores). They look at the footprint, the broken window, and the exact sequence of events. Analyzing model failures is detective work.

cards.title: Spurious Background Cues
cards.body: During error analysis of a boat detector, you notice that the model consistently misses boats on dry docks. By looking at the failures, you realize it learned a cheat: it only looks for water. This is background bias.

cards.title: Accuracy doesn't tell the whole story
cards.body: You might think a model with 99% accuracy is safe. But if that 1% error is consistently failing to spot pedestrians in red jackets because it's biased against red, that high accuracy hides a dangerous, systematic bug.

cards.title: Overall Stats vs. Error Auditing
cards.body: Comparing quantitative metrics and qualitative error analysis:

left.text:
Overall accuracy: 95%.
mAP: 0.72.
Tells you *how often* the model fails.

right.text:
Inspect specific wrong images.
Categorize failure modes.
Tells you *why* the model fails.

exercises.prompt: What is the primary focus of failure case analysis?
exercises.explain: Failure case analysis inspects incorrect samples to discover why the model made mistakes.

options.text: Qualitatively inspecting and categorizing the model's incorrect predictions
Writing code to compress the model parameters
Increasing the learning rate to ignore errors
Calculating overall accuracy on the training set

exercises.prompt: True or False: Overall accuracy is sufficient to prove a vision model is safe for deployment in high-stakes settings like medical diagnosis.
exercises.explain: False. High overall accuracy can hide dangerous, systematic failure modes on rare but critical classes.
Background bias happens when a model relies on surrounding textures rather than the target object itself.
exercises.sentence: If a model relies on background water to detect boats, it has developed a background ___.

exercises.prompt: Which of these is a qualitative failure analysis step?
exercises.explain: Viewing misclassified images directly is the core qualitative step of error analysis.

options.text: Opening and viewing images that the model incorrectly classified as false positives
Calculating the total number of floating-point operations (FLOPs)
Dividing the test dataset into five equal folds

exercises.prompt: You are building a package scanner. Your model has 98% overall accuracy. However, when inspecting failures, you find it misses 80% of packages wrapped in blue bubble wrap. Why is this finding important?

options.text: It reveals a systematic failure on a specific data slice (blue wrap) that is masked by the high overall accuracy score.
It shows that blue bubble wrap changes the height and width of the images.
It proves that blue bubble wrap causes GPU hardware memory leaks.
It indicates that the model is ready to be exported to ONNX format without changes.

exercises.explain: High-level metrics can hide terrible performance on minor data slices. Spotting this allows you to balance your dataset and fix the bug.
